title: Setting Up the Code Sandbox
concept: Configuring the isolated environment to execute code safely without host privileges.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of a code sandbox?

options.text: To execute code safely and without direct system access

exercises.explain: A code sandbox is used to run code safely and without risking your computer.

cards.title: What is a Code Sandbox?
cards.body: A code sandbox is a special space where you can write and run code without affecting your main system.

cards.title: Code Sandbox Definition
cards.body: A code sandbox is an isolated environment that executes code securely and prevents harmful actions to the host machine.

cards.title: Sandbox Analogy
cards.body: Think of a code sandbox like a playroom where children can play safely, while the rest of the house stays clean and undamaged.

cards.title: Example of a Code Sandbox
cards.body: An example of a code sandbox is an online platform like CodePen or Replit, where you can write and test code without any risk.

cards.title: Myth: Sandboxes are for Advanced Users Only
cards.body: Many think that code sandboxes are too complex for beginners, but they are actually user-friendly and designed for everyone!

cards.title: Sandbox vs. Regular Environment
cards.body: In a sandbox, your code runs separately from your system, reducing risk.

right.text: Code can affect the entire system.

exercises.prompt: What is one key benefit of using a code sandbox?

options.text: It keeps the main system safe from code mistakes.
It can install software on your computer.
It requires special hardware to use.

exercises.explain: Using a code sandbox keeps your main system safe from any accidental damage or errors.

exercises.prompt: A code sandbox can directly access your computer's files.
exercises.explain: False! A code sandbox is designed to isolate your code, preventing it from accessing the main system's files.

exercises.prompt: A code sandbox helps to execute code without risking the ______ of the host system.
exercises.sentence: A code sandbox helps to execute code without risking the safety of the host system.
exercises.explain: A code sandbox allows code execution with protection, ensuring the safety of the host system.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements is true about code sandboxes?

options.text: They can cause serious system crashes.
They keep your main system safe while you code.
They require complex installations to work.

exercises.explain: True! Code sandboxes provide a controlled environment, ensuring coding mistakes don't harm your main system.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a feature of a code sandbox?

options.text: Ability to run code without host privileges.
Access to the host system's file system directly.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'Access to the host system's file system directly.' Code sandboxes are intentionally designed to prevent this for safety.
